proof_trace: The only NULL guard in DestroyRandomInfoThreadSet is assert(random_info != NULL) at line 45, which is compiled out in release builds (NDEBUG). AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() returns NULL on AcquireQuantumMemory failure, and all 6 external callers (fx.c:402, statistic.c:774, statistic.c:881, effect.c:3857, threshold.c:2149, fx.c:4562) pass the result directly to DestroyRandomInfoThreadSet without a NULL check. In release builds, the loop at line 46 dereferences random_info[i] with no guard.
attack_scenario: An attacker submits a crafted image to trigger AddNoiseImage, EvaluateImage, SpreadImage, or RandomThresholdImage under memory pressure; AcquireQuantumMemory fails causing AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet to return NULL; in non-OpenMP builds the NULL pointer reaches DestroyRandomInfoThreadSet and the unguarded loop dereference causes a crash (DoS).

proof_trace: `AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet()` explicitly returns NULL on allocation failure (random-private.h:66-67, 73), but `AddNoiseImage` performs no NULL check before dereferencing `random_info[0]` at line 326 (OpenMP path) and `random_info[id]` at line 377. The cleanup call at line 402 also invokes `assert(random_info != NULL)` inside `DestroyRandomInfoThreadSet`, compounding the failure.
attack_scenario: An attacker submits a crafted or large image to an ImageMagick `-noise` operation; under memory pressure `AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet` returns NULL, which is immediately dereferenced at fx.c:326 or fx.c:377, causing a NULL pointer dereference crash (DoS).

proof_trace: GetBlobFileHandle() returns image->blob->file_info.file, which OpenBlob() legally sets to NULL on success when gzip/bzip2 magic bytes are detected or memory-mapping is used. coders/dot.c:140-142 passes the return value directly to agread() without a NULL check; agread(NULL) causes a NULL pointer dereference. Secondary unchecked callers exist in dps.c:217,230,478,482 and svg.c:3580; info.c:191 is safe because IdentifyImage() internally falls back to stdout on NULL.
attack_scenario: Attacker supplies a file whose first three bytes are the gzip magic (0x1F 0x8B 0x08) with a .dot extension; OpenBlob detects the magic, closes file_info.file, sets it to NULL, and returns MagickTrue, after which ReadDOTImage calls agread(NULL), crashing the process.

proof_trace: AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() explicitly returns NULL on two failure paths (random-private.h:67, :73) but its return value at threshold.c:2075 is never NULL-checked before the first dereference at line 2078 (random_info[0] under OpenMP) and line 2127 (random_info[id] in the pixel loop). No enforcement mechanism exists: the same unchecked pattern is repeated across fx.c, statistic.c, and effect.c, confirming systemic missing NULL checks rather than intentional design.
attack_scenario: An attacker submits a crafted image (or floods the server with concurrent image requests) to exhaust heap memory; when AcquireRandomInfo() or AcquireQuantumMemory fails inside AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet(), the function returns NULL, and the subsequent dereference of random_info[0] or random_info[id] causes a NULL pointer dereference crash (DoS).

proof_trace: CloneKernelInfo() is called three times (lines 2336, 2341, 2346) and its return value is passed directly to RotateKernelInfo() (lines 2337, 2342, 2347) without any NULL check. CloneKernelInfo() returns NULL on allocation failure (AcquireMagickMemory or AcquireAlignedMemory), and RotateKernelInfo() immediately dereferences kernel->next at line 4231, causing a NULL pointer dereference crash. Other call sites in the same file (lines 3769–3770, 4154–4155) correctly check for NULL before use, confirming this is an oversight.
attack_scenario: An attacker submits a crafted image to a service using ImageMagick with a kernel specification containing the '<' modifier (mirror expansion), triggering ExpandMirrorKernelInfo(); under attacker-induced memory pressure (e.g., via a large image allocation), CloneKernelInfo() returns NULL and RotateKernelInfo() dereferences it, crashing the process (DoS).

proof_trace: CloneKernelInfo() can return NULL on memory allocation failure (AcquireMagickMemory at L2223 or AcquireAlignedMemory at L2231). In ExpandRotateKernelInfo (L2424-2426), the return value is used immediately as RotateKernelInfo(clone, angle) and SameKernelInfo(kernel, clone) with no NULL guard; both callees dereference the pointer at their first line (L4231 and L2393 respectively). Other callers of CloneKernelInfo in the same file (L3769, L4154) properly check for NULL, confirming this omission is an oversight.
attack_scenario: An attacker provides a crafted image processed via a morphology operation with a kernel rotation flag (> or @); under memory pressure the CloneKernelInfo call inside ExpandRotateKernelInfo returns NULL, which is immediately dereferenced by RotateKernelInfo or SameKernelInfo, causing a NULL pointer dereference crash (denial of service).

proof_trace: `AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet()` returns NULL on allocation failure (random-private.h:66-67), but `EvaluateImages()` assigns the result to `random_info` at L506 with no NULL check before dereferencing `random_info[0]` at L511 (Median path) and L613 (else path). By contrast, the analogous `evaluate_pixels` allocation at L493 has an explicit NULL guard (L494-500), confirming this is an oversight.
attack_scenario: Attacker submits a crafted large image (e.g., via ImageMagick CLI or API) to trigger memory exhaustion; `AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet()` returns NULL, and the subsequent `random_info[0]` dereference at L511/L613 causes a NULL-pointer dereference crash (DoS).

proof_trace: AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() returns NULL on allocation failure (random-private.h:66-67, 72-73), but SpreadImage() performs no NULL check before dereferencing random_info[0] at effect.c:3804 (OpenMP path) and random_info[id] at effect.c:3833 (pixel loop). Additionally, DestroyRandomInfoThreadSet() at effect.c:3857 asserts random_info != NULL, so any path through the function crashes if the allocation fails.
attack_scenario: An attacker submits an image processed with the -spread effect under controlled memory pressure (e.g., resource limits or concurrent allocations); AcquireRandomInfoThreadSet() returns NULL, and the immediate dereference of random_info[0] or random_info[id] causes a NULL pointer dereference crash (DoS).
